checks.a_analysis: The output content accurately reflects the text found in the source document. No fabricated sentences or sections were found. The section title 'Slide Number 3' for the third page is an acceptable AI-generated heading for navigation as the source page lacks a textual heading, which is permitted under 'Acceptable Changes'.
The source document contains no phone numbers, email addresses, or URLs. The output correctly reflects this absence of contact information.
All three pages from the source PDF are represented in the JSON output as distinct sections. No entire pages or primary document sections are missing.
The source document contains one table (Table 5) on the third page. The table in the output correctly includes the header rows and the single data row present in the source.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Significant semantic information is missing from the maps on pages 1 and 2. Specifically, the map legends, which are essential for interpreting the data, have been omitted from the text extraction. On Page 1, the 'Status' legend (approx. 22 words) explaining DEM processing states is missing. On Page 2, the 'Phase & Quality' legend (approx. 25 words) detailing years and quality levels for five phases is missing. Given the total word count per page, these omissions exceed 25% of the textual content for these sections.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 1 Map Legend: 'Status', 'Reprojected from UTM meter to State Plane feet *', 'DEMs in Que', 'DEMs Processing (3, 5, 10, 20, 50ft)', 'DEMs Created (3, 10, & 20ft Tiled)'. Page 2 Map Legend: 'Phase & Quality', 'Phase 1 (2020) - QL 1', 'Phase 2 (2020) - QL 1', 'Phase 3 (2015) - QL 2', 'Phase 4 (2016) - QL 1', 'Phase 5 (2017) - QL 1'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The text content for the map legends is entirely missing from the element lists in sections 1 and 2.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The extracted text preserves the meaning of the source material. Wording in the paragraphs and table matches the source exactly, including technical details such as cell sizes and dates.

checks.a_analysis: The output faithfully represents the content of the three slides. Each section contains unique text corresponding to the respective page of the source. While the DPS logos are repeated in the source (appearing on every slide), their inclusion in each section of the output is a correct reflection of the source material and not an error of duplication. No paragraphs or text blocks are repeated more times than they appear in the original document.

The heading hierarchy is logical and accessible. The primary slide titles serve as section titles (effectively H1). In Section 1, the sub-title 'NC Hurricane Florence...' is identified as a heading (H2), which correctly nests within the section's hierarchy. There are no skipped levels or incorrectly nested headings. The use of an AI-generated title 'Slide Number 3' for the third section is an acceptable change for accessibility.

Table 5 in Section 3 is correctly converted. The complex header structure, where 'Delivery dates' spans two columns in the first row, is accurately captured using the 'colspan' property. The subsequent header row correctly defines the three distinct columns. The data rows align perfectly with the headers as shown in the source PDF.

The reading order in the output follows the logical flow of the source document's slides. On Page 1, the title, map data, and footnotes follow the visual sequence. On Page 2, the map annotations are captured in a sequence that makes sense for the slide's layout. Page 3 correctly orders the project timeline text, the table, and the concluding note.

The bulleted point 'Project Timeline:' on Page 3 of the source is correctly identified and converted as a list element in Section 3 of the output. The structure of the list item correctly encapsulates both the bolded term and the following description.
There are no functional hyperlinks in the source document. The output correctly reflects this by not including any 'link' elements or URLs.

checks.a_analysis: The source document is a map-based status report where the primary organization and communication of information occur through large, central maps on the first two pages. In the HTML output, these maps are entirely missing, resulting in a single-column text layout. This fundamentally alters the visual organization and flow of the document, as the spatial context provided by the maps is lost. The text that was originally contained within callout boxes on the maps is now just floating text, disconnected from its geographical references.
checks.b_evidence_source: Pages 1 and 2 feature large, central maps ('April 9, 2024 NCEM Status' and 'North Carolina LiDAR Quality Level By Phase') that serve as the primary organizational structure for the data presented.
checks.b_evidence_output: The maps are absent, replaced by a vertical stack of logos and text. The visual flow does not match the source's intent of a map-based presentation.

checks.a_analysis: Table 5 from Page 3 is successfully rendered in the HTML. It maintains a clear table structure with distinct borders, a shaded header row, and aligned columns. The nested header structure ('Delivery dates' spanning two sub-columns) is correctly interpreted and displayed, making the data easily readable for a sighted user.

Several meaningful and content-critical images are missing from the HTML output. Specifically, the map on Page 1, the map on Page 2, and the technical diagram of the airplane on Page 2 are all absent. While some logos are present, the loss of these maps and diagrams constitutes a major failure in visual fidelity as they contain the core data of the report. A solid green rectangle appears on the second page where a map should be, indicating a failed rendering or missing asset.
checks.b_evidence_source: The map on Page 1 ('April 9, 2024 NCEM Status'), the map on Page 2 ('North Carolina LiDAR Quality Level By Phase'), and the airplane data collection diagram on Page 2.
checks.b_evidence_output: These three meaningful visual elements are missing. A large green placeholder box is shown instead of the map on the second page.

checks.a_analysis: The core content (text and data tables) is not duplicated in the HTML output. While logos are repeated at the beginning of each section, this aligns with the source PDF's structure where logos appear on every page/slide header and footer. There are no instances of paragraphs or tables being rendered multiple times within the same context.
